title: Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup

seo.title: Authentic Ribollita Recipe: Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Ribollita, the classic Tuscan twice-boiled soup. This hearty Italian recipe features vegetables, beans, and bread, perfect for a comforting meal.

seo.h1: Authentic Ribollita Recipe: Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup
h2Headings: What is Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup?

seo.imageAlt: Bowl of authentic Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup with crusty bread and olive oil drizzle.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a warm, hearty bowl of traditional Ribollita, a classic Tuscan twice-boiled soup, perfect for a comforting meal.

description: Ribollita is a heartwarming, rustic Tuscan soup, renowned for its "twice-boiled" preparation and rich, comforting flavors. This traditional peasant dish transforms simple, wholesome ingredients like vegetables, beans, and stale bread into a surprisingly elegant and deeply satisfying meal. It's a true taste of Italian Nonna's kitchen, perfect for chilly evenings.

ingredients.notes: rinsed and drained, reserving some liquid
or other rustic, crusty bread, sliced or torn

instructions.title: Prepare the Soffritto Base
instructions.text: Heat the olive oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Cook gently for about 8-10 minutes, until the vegetables are softened and translucent, but not browned.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic. Add the crushed tomatoes, stirring well to combine with the soffritto. Let it simmer for 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Incorporate Beans and Greens
instructions.text: Add one can of rinsed cannellini beans to the pot. Mash about half of the second can of beans with a fork and add them to the pot along with the remaining whole beans. This helps thicken the soup. Stir in the chopped black kale and Savoy cabbage. Add the vegetable broth and the optional Parmesan rind. Bring the soup to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for at least 25-30 minutes, or until the greens are tender.

instructions.title: First Simmer and Bread Layer
instructions.text: Remove the Parmesan rind. Season the soup with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Take a portion of the soup (about 1-2 cups) and blend it with an immersion blender or in a regular blender until smooth, then return it to the pot. This creates a creamier texture. In individual serving bowls, layer slices of stale bread at the bottom.

instructions.title: The "Twice Boiled" Tradition
instructions.text: Ladle the hot soup over the bread in the bowls, ensuring the bread is completely submerged. Allow the soup to sit for 5-10 minutes, letting the bread soak up the flavorful broth and soften. This is the first part of the "twice-boiled" process, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Serve and Enjoy
instructions.text: For the traditional "twice-boiled" effect, you can gently reheat the soup in the pot again or even transfer the soup-soaked bread mixture back into the pot and gently simmer for another 5-10 minutes before serving. Garnish each bowl generously with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil and a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley. Serve hot.

tips: For true Tuscan flavor, use high-quality extra virgin olive oil for both cooking and drizzling at the end. It makes a significant difference in the final taste.
Authentic Ribollita often uses day-old, unsalted Tuscan bread. If unavailable, use any good quality stale rustic bread, avoiding overly flavored varieties, for the best texture.

Ribollita is designed to be "twice-boiled." For best results, prepare it a day ahead, refrigerate, and then gently reheat it on the stovetop or even bake it in a gratin dish until crusty before serving.
article: Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup is a beloved Italian Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
